channel.title: AI Playbooks on XeroToAI
channel.description: Recent content in AI Playbooks on XeroToAI

item.title: AI Tools for Restaurant Owners: The Practical Playbook
item.description:
Introduction: Why Restaurants Are Behind — and Why That’s About to Change

Running a restaurant means juggling food costs, staffing headaches, inconsistent reviews, and marketing you never have time to do properly. Most owners are still writing menu copy in a Google Doc at midnight, manually building the schedule every Sunday, and ignoring half their Google reviews because they don’t know what to say.

The honest truth: AI won’t fix your food costs or solve turnover. But it will eliminate several hours of low-skill administrative work each week — work you’re currently doing yourself or paying someone else to do badly. That’s the real value proposition here. Not transformation. Time.

item.title: The Complete AI Stack for Solo Accountants and Bookkeepers
item.description:
Introduction

If you run a solo accounting or bookkeeping practice, your days are shaped by a specific kind of cognitive load: context-switching between clients, chasing documents, writing the same explanatory emails over and over, and trying to stay current on software that changes quarterly. You’re not managing a team—you’re managing everything, including your own attention.

AI has been noisy for three years. Most of the hype has been irrelevant to your work. But something shifted in 2024 and 2025: the tools got specific enough to be genuinely useful in this vertical. Not “AI will automate accounting”—that’s still nonsense—but AI that handles the surrounding work: communication, research, document review, report drafting. The repetitive cognitive work that fills the gaps between billable hours.
